claims.statement: Effective microphysics is the reduced parameter layer that turns particle-level interactions into usable potentials, collision rates, opacity, conductivity, friction, and elastic response coefficients.

constraints: the reduction is coefficient-level, not a certified solver
particle physics contributes through effective parameters rather than direct macroscopic observables
the claim remains subordinate to the parent transport tree

sources.citation: Born and Oppenheimer (1927), Zur Quantentheorie der Molekeln, DOI 10.1002/andp.19273892002.
sources.note: Source for the microscopic-to-effective-potential reduction step.

sources.citation: McDaniels and Schmidt (2016), Next-Generation Force Fields from Symmetry-Adapted Perturbation Theory, DOI 10.1146/annurev-physchem-040215-112047.
sources.note: Source for effective interaction decomposition and force-field construction.
Source for pair interactions, external potentials, and many-body reduction.

claims.notes: This is the shared reduced-microphysics entry point for the new parent tree.

claims.statement: An effective interaction Hamiltonian captures conservative reduced interactions after microscopic degrees of freedom are eliminated, including pair potentials and explicit coupling to external fields or potentials.

constraints: the Hamiltonian must remain reduced and effective
external potentials are allowed only as explicit terms in the reduced model
dissipative response is not yet closed at this layer

sources.note: Microscopic reduction to effective molecular energies.

sources.citation: Evans (1979), The nature of the liquid-vapour interface and other topics in the statistical mechanics of non-uniform, classical fluids, DOI 10.1080/00018737900101365.
sources.note: External-potential and free-energy response context.
Many-body Hamiltonian with external potential framing.

claims.notes: This claim keeps the reduced Hamiltonian explicit before coarse-graining and dissipation.

claims.statement: The many-body Hamiltonian is the ensemble-level conservative description from which partition functions, correlation functions, and response formulas are derived before closure is imposed.

constraints: pair or effective interactions must be explicit in the reduced model
ensemble-level observables are derived before transport closure
this layer is not yet a dissipative constitutive law

sources.note: Many-body Hamiltonian, hierarchy, and external potential reduction.
Nonuniform fluid response and free-energy methods.

claims.notes: This is the shared conservative ensemble backbone for the new parent tree.

claims.statement: Kinetic theory is the reduced distribution-function description that replaces the full many-body state with evolution equations for occupancies, collision terms, and hierarchy truncations.

constraints: distribution functions or equivalent reduced moments must be explicit
collision operators or source terms must remain visible
the claim does not assert a closed transport coefficient until closure is added

sources.note: Kinetic and hierarchy semantics for many-body reduction.
Linear-response bridge into reduced transport descriptions.

claims.notes: This claim is the parent for kinetic truncation and transport closure.

claims.statement: Kinetic transport closure is the step that turns hierarchy equations into usable damping, diffusion, source, and transport coefficients for reduced macroscopic models.

constraints: the closure must expose a reduced transport coefficient set
dissipation and source terms must be explicit
the claim remains diagnostic rather than a certified kinetic solver

sources.note: Reduced response and closure context.
Kinetic-to-fluid closure semantics for plasma transport.

claims.notes: This claim is the practical closure layer used by both granular and plasma branches.

claims.statement: Constitutive response is the reduced bulk relation between stress, strain, pressure, strength, viscosity, friction, and other material closure variables that follows from the effective microphysics layer.

constraints: the closure must be bulk-level rather than microscopic
equation-of-state and strength terms may coexist as separate regime variables
the claim remains a closure relation, not a certified material model

sources.note: External-potential and free-energy response context.
Granular aggregate response and effective material behavior.

sources.citation: Holsapple, equilibrium configurations of solid cohesionless bodies.
sources.note: Cohesionless-body closure and strength-versus-self-gravity context.

claims.notes: This is the shared closure node for strength, elasticity, viscosity, and friction.

claims.statement: Plasma transport closure is the reduced description that packages collision frequency, conductivity, resistivity, opacity, and source or damping terms into a usable continuum or MHD-level model.

constraints: the closure must remain a reduced plasma transport model
collisional and kinetic ingredients must be visible in the reduction
the claim remains diagnostic and not a certified flare or coronal solver

sources.note: Observable context for solar plasma transport and waves.

sources.citation: Klimchuk, Nanoflare Heating of Solar and Stellar Coronae.
sources.note: Heating and transport semantics in stellar coronae.
Kinetic-to-fluid plasma closure semantics.

claims.notes: This is the plasma-specific closure node for the shared parent tree.

claims.statement: Collective observable response is the endpoint where closure-level microphysics produces measurable shape, spin, tidal, wave, flare, or helioseismic outputs.

constraints: the response must be derived from a closed or reduced closure model
the output remains regime-specific and not a universal solver claim
the claim is a bridge into observable lanes rather than a final certified product

sources.note: Nonuniform fluid response and external-potential minimization.
Observable context for wave response in the solar branch.
Observable shape response in the granular branch.

claims.notes: This is the shared endpoint for the new parent tree and its downstream branches.
